odeProperty.value: English in Europe: Designing an English Magazine to Promote Linguistic Diversity
2nd ESO English Project about linguistic diversity in Europe

El alumnado de 2 eso trabajará de forma cooperativa en la elaboración de una Revista digital informativa en inglés que refleje el papel del inglés en Europa y la importancia de la diversidad lingüística.
creative commons: attribution - non commercial - share alike 4.0

odePagStructure.blockName: English in Europe: Designing an English Magazine to Promote Linguistic Diversity”.

odeComponent.htmlView:
Welcome to our project!

In this project, we are going to explore linguistic diversity in Europe and the role of English as a lingua franca in different areas such as education, tourism, culture and digital communication.

During the project, you will work in groups to create an informative digital magazine in English. To do this, you will search for reliable information, select relevant content, use digital tools and present your work orally to the rest of the class.

The main aim is to learn English in a practical, cooperative and meaningful way, while developing your digital competence and your respect for linguistic and cultural diversity.Image created by Guadalupe Luque
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778260800517-yccksx41o","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Welcome to our project!

In this project, we are going to explore linguistic diversity in Europe and the role of English as a lingua franca in different areas such as education, tourism, culture and digital communication.

During the project, you will work in groups to create an informative digital magazine in English. To do this, you will search for reliable information, select relevant content, use digital tools and present your work orally to the rest of the class.

The main aim is to learn English in a practical, cooperative and meaningful way, while developing your digital competence and your respect for linguistic and cultural diversity.Image created by Guadalupe Luque 

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
This learning situation is designed for 2nd year of Compulsory Secondary Education in the subject of English as a Foreign Language.

The main topic of the project is linguistic diversity in Europe and the role of English as a lingua franca in different contexts. Students will research European countries, languages, the use of English, culture and international communication.

The project will take approximately 12–14 lessons, organised into three main stages:

Initial stage: project presentation, activation of prior knowledge and group organisation.
Development stage: information search, analysis, selection of content and writing in English.
Final stage: creation of the digital magazine, oral presentation and assessment.
The methodology is based on project-based learning, cooperative work, meaningful learning, the use of digital tools and formative assessment through rubrics.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778261225070-e16sl1a7i","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

This learning situation is designed for 2nd year of Compulsory Secondary Education in the subject of English as a Foreign Language.

The main topic of the project is linguistic diversity in Europe and the role of English as a lingua franca in different contexts. Students will research European countries, languages, the use of English, culture and international communication.

The project will take approximately 12–14 lessons, organised into three main stages:

Initial stage: project presentation, activation of prior knowledge and group organisation.
Development stage: information search, analysis, selection of content and writing in English.
Final stage: creation of the digital magazine, oral presentation and assessment.
The methodology is based on project-based learning, cooperative work, meaningful learning, the use of digital tools and formative assessment through rubrics.

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
The final challenge is to create a collaborative digital magazine in English about linguistic diversity in Europe.

Each group will create one section of the magazine. This section must include clear, visual and well-organised information about a European country, its languages, the use of English and some relevant cultural aspects.

The magazine section must include:An attractive title.
Information about a European country.
Languages spoken in that country.
The role of English in that context.
Interesting facts or cultural curiosities.
Images, maps or icons with an appropriate licence.
Short texts written in English.
Credits for the sources used.
A final reflection on the importance of linguistic diversity.
The product will preferably be designed with Canva or another digital tool approved by the teacher.Image created b Guadalupe Luque
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778261348330-6rxeodkg2","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

The final challenge is to create a collaborative digital magazine in English about linguistic diversity in Europe.

\n

Each group will create one section of the magazine. This section must include clear, visual and well-organised information about a European country, its languages, the use of English and some relevant cultural aspects.

\n

The magazine section must include:An attractive title.
Information about a European country.
Languages spoken in that country.
The role of English in that context.
Interesting facts or cultural curiosities.
Images, maps or icons with an appropriate licence.
Short texts written in English.
Credits for the sources used.
A final reflection on the importance of linguistic diversity.
The product will preferably be designed with Canva or another digital tool approved by the teacher.Image created b Guadalupe Luque

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
During this project, we will use the internet and digital tools. For this reason, we must protect our personal data and respect everyone’s digital rights.

Basic rules:

  • Do not publish your full name, address, telephone number, personal email or any private information.
  • Use only your school account when working online.
  • Do not share your passwords.
  • Do not upload photos of yourself or your classmates without permission.
  • Do not publish other people’s work as if it were your own.
  • Use copyright-free images or images with Creative Commons licences.
  • Always cite the sources of the information, images, videos or audio files you use.
    Use respectful language in all digital spaces.
  • Before publishing any content, check it with your group and with your teacher.
  • Remember that everything you do online is part of your digital footprint.

With this project, we will learn how to use technology in a safe, critical, responsible and respectful way.

Read the following infographic carefully before continuing with the project.

Author: Guadalupe Luque López. Licence: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778261831362-9cihomnme","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

During this project, we will use the internet and digital tools. For this reason, we must protect our personal data and respect everyone’s digital rights.

Basic rules:

  • Do not publish your full name, address, telephone number, personal email or any private information.
  • Use only your school account when working online.
  • Do not share your passwords.
  • Do not upload photos of yourself or your classmates without permission.
  • Do not publish other people’s work as if it were your own.
  • Use copyright-free images or images with Creative Commons licences.
  • Always cite the sources of the information, images, videos or audio files you use.
    Use respectful language in all digital spaces.
  • Before publishing any content, check it with your group and with your teacher.
  • Remember that everything you do online is part of your digital footprint.

With this project, we will learn how to use technology in a safe, critical, responsible and respectful way.

Read the following infographic carefully before continuing with the project.

Author: Guadalupe Luque López. Licence: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Resources for Students
In this space, you will find a selection of resources to help you complete the project: websites to research European languages, online dictionaries, tools to find copyright-free images, Canva tutorials and materials to improve your writing in English.

Enlace a padlet: https://padlet.com/gluqlop768/digital-magazine-about-linguistic-diversity-in-europe-w1tjcv1vsifj8wtr

Resources for Teachers
This collection is designed for teachers. It includes methodological resources, digital tools, project examples, materials about linguistic diversity, guidelines to work on digital competence and assessment resources.

Enlace a Symbaloo: https://www.symbaloo.com/shared/AAAAAwN5ewQAA41_W3SWiw==

Resources for Students
In this space, you will find a selection of resources to help you complete the project: websites to research European languages, online dictionaries, tools to find copyright-free images, Canva tutorials and materials to improve your writing in English.

Enlace a padlet: https://padlet.com/gluqlop768/digital-magazine-about-linguistic-diversity-in-europe-w1tjcv1vsifj8wtr

Resources for Teachers
This collection is designed for teachers. It includes methodological resources, digital tools, project examples, materials about linguistic diversity, guidelines to work on digital competence and assessment resources.

Enlace a Symbaloo: https://www.symbaloo.com/shared/AAAAAwN5ewQAA41_W3SWiw==

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
This timeline shows the main steps of the project. It will help students understand what they have to do in each lesson and how the final product will be created gradually.

Timeline created by Guadalupe Luque . Licence: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778264309053-rgx9ojgxb","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

This timeline shows the main steps of the project. It will help students understand what they have to do in each lesson and how the final product will be created gradually.

Timeline created by Guadalupe Luque . Licence: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView: Assessment in this project will be continuous, formative and varied. It will not only focus on the final digital magazine, but also on the whole learning process. The aim is to collect evidence of students’ progress, detect possible difficulties, offer personalised feedback and help students become more aware of their own learning. For this reason, different assessment tools will be used such as: a writing rubric and a self-assessment target diagram.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778266469728-a1044ayml","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Assessment in this project will be continuous, formative and varied. It will not only focus on the final digital magazine, but also on the whole learning process. The aim is to collect evidence of students’ progress, detect possible difficulties, offer personalised feedback and help students become more aware of their own learning. For this reason, different assessment tools will be used such as: a writing rubric and a self-assessment target diagram.

\n

\n

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView: Rubric created by Guadalupe Luque López for educational use Licence CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

\n

Rubric created by Guadalupe Luque López for educational use Licence CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

odeComponent.htmlView:
Didactic guide

This section includes the main didactic information of the project. 

Here you can find: 

  • the sequence of tasks
  • the assessment criteria related to those tasks
  • and some methodological notes.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778269479946-h5un48r0j","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Didactic guide

This section includes the main didactic information of the project. 

Here you can find: 

  • the sequence of tasks
  • the assessment criteria related to those tasks
  • and some methodological notes. 
","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView: This sequence has been designed taking into account previous reflections on teaching practice, the need to use realistic digital tools and the importance of planning the project in a flexible and coherent way.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778269637275-s7m1wnfvu","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

This sequence has been designed taking into account previous reflections on teaching practice, the need to use realistic digital tools and the importance of planning the project in a flexible and coherent way. 

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
The project is designed for 2nd ESO students in the subject of English.
The final product is a digital magazine in English about linguistic diversity in Europe.
The project combines whole class work, pair work and small group work.
The main digital tools used are Padlet, Liveworksheets, Google Classroom and Canva.
The activities are organised in a progressive way, from comprehension and vocabulary work to the creation of the final product. 

Didactic guide created by Guadalupe Luque López for educatoinal use Licence CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778270351885-pz55zlhjw","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

The project is designed for 2nd ESO students in the subject of English.
The final product is a digital magazine in English about linguistic diversity in Europe.
The project combines whole class work, pair work and small group work.
The main digital tools used are Padlet, Liveworksheets, Google Classroom and Canva.
The activities are organised in a progressive way, from comprehension and vocabulary work to the creation of the final product. 

Didactic guide created by Guadalupe Luque López for educatoinal use Licence CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
iThe project has been designed according to the principles of Universal Design for Learning, with the aim of offering different ways to access information, participate and express learning.

Multiple means of representation
Oral and written explanations.
Visual examples.
Work templates.
Vocabulary glossaries.
Sentence starters in English.
Previously selected digital resources.


Multiple means of action and expression
Students can provide information through texts, images, maps or oral presentations.
Use of accessible digital tools.
Cooperative work with defined roles.
Guided revision before the final submission.
Oral presentation supported by visual materials.


Multiple means of engagement
Choice of country or language, when possible.
Teamwork.
Creative and realistic final product.
Connection with cultural and linguistic diversity.
Self-assessment and final reflection.
Specific support measures
Templates with useful language structures.
Reduction of text length for students with difficulties.
Peer support.
Extra time if needed.
Possibility of recording the oral presentation beforehand.
Step-by-step revision of the work.


This section has been created by Guadalupe Luque López for educational use Licence CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
odeComponent.jsonProperties:
{"ideviceId":"idevice-1778268972614-8qg65khhr","textInfoDurationInput":"","textInfoDurationTextInput":"Duración","textInfoParticipantsInput":"","textInfoParticipantsTextInput":"Agrupamiento","textTextarea":"

Multiple means of representation
Oral and written explanations.
Visual examples.
Work templates.
Vocabulary glossaries.
Sentence starters in English.
Previously selected digital resources.


Multiple means of action and expression
Students can provide information through texts, images, maps or oral presentations.
Use of accessible digital tools.
Cooperative work with defined roles.
Guided revision before the final submission.
Oral presentation supported by visual materials.


Multiple means of engagement
Choice of country or language, when possible.
Teamwork.
Creative and realistic final product.
Connection with cultural and linguistic diversity.
Self-assessment and final reflection.
Specific support measures
Templates with useful language structures.
Reduction of text length for students with difficulties.
Peer support.
Extra time if needed.
Possibility of recording the oral presentation beforehand.
Step-by-step revision of the work.


This section has been created by Guadalupe Luque López for educational use Licence CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.


","textFeedbackInput":"Mostrar retroalimentación","textFeedbackTextarea":""}

odeComponent.htmlView:
Título
English in Europe: Designing an English Magazine to Promote Linguistic Diversity

Autora:
Guadalupe Luque López

Nivel educativo
2º ESO

Materia
Lengua Extranjera: Inglés

Descripción
Recurso educativo digital creado con eXeLearning para desarrollar una situación de aprendizaje basada en la creación de una revista digital en inglés sobre la diversidad lingüística en Europa y el papel del inglés como lengua franca.

Licencia
Creative Commons 4.0 Internacional, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

Uso educativo

Este material ha sido creado con fines educativos y puede ser reutilizado y adaptado para usos no comerciales, siempre que se reconozca la autoría y se mantenga la misma licencia.
